Short Description: My childhood home, where snowy winters and the nearby Chréa Mountains shaped some of my earliest memories.
Personal Memory: I lived in Algiers as a child, so I have few photographs from that period, but I remember loving it there. Every couple of winters, the city would wake beneath a light blanket of snow, something that always felt magical. Some of my strongest memories are of driving into the Chréa Mountains, where the snow transformed the landscape and made winter feel like an adventure.
Private Notes: Few or no childhood photographs. Look for family albums and old photographs of Chréa.

Short Description: An extraordinary Roman archaeological site overlooking the Mediterranean Sea.
Personal Memory: We visited Tipasa almost every year. Walking through streets that had existed for nearly two thousand years always made me imagine the ordinary lives once lived there. Even as a child, the ruins never felt like piles of old stones. They felt alive, and they made history tangible in a way books could not.
Private Notes: Annual childhood visits. Search family albums for photographs.
